not_found.title: Page not found
not_found.heading: 404: Page not found
not_found.message: The page you're looking for doesn't exist or may have been moved.
service_description: This is a {{serverLink}} of {{imageWorkerLink}} and {{textWorkerLink}} by the volunteer organization {{haidraLink}}. If you like this service, consider helping us continue to make it possible by {{joinHordeLink}}!
more_information: For more information about the service, visit the {{faqLink}}. You can learn more about Haidra (the team behind it) at {{haidraLink}}, and you can also follow the {{developerBlogLink}} for the latest updates on features and our status. Have questions? Join our active {{discordLink}}!
latest_news.title: Latest news
latest_news.subtitle: Updates, announcements, and developments from the AI Horde community.
standard_images.tooltip.line2: Megapixelsteps = megapixels × steps. Steps linearly increase the length of a job. For example, a 20 step job takes twice as long as a 10 step job.
standard_images.tooltip.line3: 1 standard image = megapixelsteps / 20 (assuming ~20 steps per typical image)
mps.tooltip.line2: Megapixelsteps = megapixels × steps. One megapixel equals a 1024×1024 image.

stats.text_queue.label: Queue
stats.interrogation_queue.label: Queue
stats.realtime: You can see detailed horde activity (including past stats) on {{grafanaLink}}
faq_subtitle: Find answers to common questions about AI Horde, from how it works to earning kudos and everything in between. For live help and discussions, join our {{discordLink}}!
quickstart.register_account: First register an account which will generate an API key. Store that key somewhere safe - treat it like a password.
quickstart.try_frontend_description: The easiest way to use AI Horde is through one of our user-friendly frontends. We recommend starting with ArtBot - a popular web-based client that's perfect for beginners.
quickstart.discord_cta_description: Join our {{discordLink}} - it's the best and most active place to ask questions, get support, and connect with the AI Horde community!
quickstart.anonymous_api_key: If you do not want to register, you can use {{anonymousApiKey}} as an API key to connect anonymously. However, anonymous accounts have the lowest priority when there are too many concurrent requests!
quickstart.explanation_api_key: To increase your priority you will need a unique API key and then to increase your
guis.description.horde_ng: HordeNG aims to be very simple to use for beginners, while allowing the full set of options for advanced users.
guis_page.title: AI Horde Frontends
guis_page.description: Explore our collection of user interfaces for generating images and text with AI Horde. Choose from web-based, desktop, and mobile applications to find the perfect tool for your needs.
guis_page.no_results: No frontends match your selected filters.
guis_and_tools_page.title: AI Horde GUIs and Tools
guis_and_tools_page.description: Explore our collection of user interfaces and developer tools for AI Horde. From web-based clients to desktop applications, mobile apps, SDKs, plugins, and bots - find everything you need to work with AI Horde.
guis_and_tools_page.intro_title: New to AI Horde? Here's what you'll find:
guis_and_tools_page.intro_paragraph_1: AI Horde is a community-powered network where volunteers share their computers to generate AI images and text for free.
guis_and_tools_page.intro_guis_desc: Easy-to-use applications for creating AI images and text. This includes web pages and mobile apps. Perfect if you just want to start making content.
guis_and_tools_page.intro_workers_desc: Software that lets you contribute your computer's power to help others. Earn kudos (priority points) by helping the community. Learn more in our FAQ.
guis_and_tools_page.intro_tools_desc: SDKs, libraries, and plugins for developers who want to integrate AI Horde into their own projects.
guis_and_tools_page.category_guis: Web, mobile, and desktop apps for creating AI images and text
guis_and_tools_page.category_workers: Volunteer your GPU and earn priority in the queue
guis_and_tools_page.category_tools: SDKs, libraries, and plugins for integration
guis_and_tools_page.intro_paragraph_2: Most users start with a GUI/Frontend. We recommend ArtBot for beginners - it's web-based, so there's nothing to install. Use the filters below to find the perfect tool for your needs. Have questions? Check our FAQ.
guis_and_tools_page.worker_cta_title: Want to help power the Horde?
guis_and_tools_page.worker_cta_descripti: All the AI generation on this platform is provided by volunteers sharing their GPU power. Become a worker and earn kudos while helping make free AI accessible to everyone. Or, make a financial contribution to support the community.
guis_and_tools_page.no_results: No items match your selected filters.
tools_section_description: Discover developer tools, plugins, SDKs, and bots for AI Horde integration.
homepage.intro.tagline: A free, community-powered generation service: volunteers share spare computer power so anyone can generate images and text.
homepage.intro.this_site: This site helps you choose a frontend, contribute compute, or integrate the REST API; it doesn’t generate content by itself.
homepage.features.free_text: Use a website (like ArtBot), bot or app to generate, with no coding or setup required.
homepage.features.community_text: Run a worker and share idle GPU/CPU power to help others and earn kudos.
homepage.features.distributed_text: See workers, throughput, and queue depth in real time.
homepage.features.nolockin_text: Integrate AI Horde into apps and tools with an open API and no vendor lock-in.
homepage_guis.title: Frontends (Websites, Apps) and Tools
homepage_guis.description: Access AI Horde through a variety of user-friendly interfaces and tools. From web browsers to desktop applications, mobile apps and SDKs, we offer multiple ways to generate images and text with AI. Browse our collection of frontends and tools to find the one that works best for you.
transfer.title: Transfer kudos
transfer.visit_old_pre: If you would like to log in using one of the account providers (Google, Discord, Github), you might need to use
transfer.visit_old_link_text: the old version of the transfer page
transfer.target_will_validate: Username will be validated before transfering
transfer.source_api_key.description: Type the API key that belongs to your user
invalid_api_key: The api key is not valid
transfer.kudos_amount.description: How many kudos do you want {{user}} to receive
transfer.success: The kudos have been successfully transferred!
transfer.error: There was an error while transferring the kudos, please try again later.
transfer.too_many_kudos: Eh, you don't have that many kudos
transfer.donate: or donate them to an educator account:
educators.explanation: Educator accounts are special accounts made available to teachers and educational institutions for classroom use of AI Horde.
profile.title: Profile
profile.login_description: Enter your API key to view your account information.
profile.no_auth_warning: Note: Your API key is only used to securely fetch your profile data and is stored locally in your browser.
profile.evaluating_kudos_tooltip: Evaluating Kudos from generations and uptime. When this reaches the threshold, you become trusted.
profile.low_kudos_info_message: Running a worker is the best way to earn kudos! However, kudos are not required to use the horde - everyone can use it for free.
profile.no_workers_match_filters: No workers match the current filters.
profile.generations.purpose: This page shows your active generations - those that are still being processed or waiting in the queue. This is provided mostly so you can find orphaned generations. The best way to manage generations is to use a frontend like ArtBot, which shows all your generations (active and completed) in one place and allows you to easily view results, re-roll, or share.
profile.generations.warning_body: Generated images and text are deleted from the server after 20 minutes. Refreshing the page will cause any displayed results to be lost. Download or save anything you want to keep.
profile.generations.field.prompt_placeho: Describe the image you want to generate...
profile.generations.not_possible: No workers available to fulfil this request. The model or parameters may not be supported by any active worker.
profile.generations.not_found_explanatio: This generation was not found on the server (HTTP 404). This usually means it was cancelled, has already completed and expired, or the tracked ID comes from a stale server cache.
profile.shared_keys.description: Create generation-only API keys to share your priority and kudos with friends and family, with no separate accounts needed. Shared keys use your priority and kudos, can have optional limits and expiry dates, and cannot transfer kudos or perform other account operations.
profile.shared_keys.cache_notice: Changes can take up to 5 minutes to show due to server caching.
profile.shared_keys.none: You have no shared keys yet.
profile.shared_keys.form_identity_descri: Give this key a label you can recognize quickly when you come back to manage or revoke it.
profile.shared_keys.form_access_descript: Define how much of your account this key can spend and how long it should stay active.
profile.shared_keys.form_limits_descript: Set ceilings for image size, step count, and text output. Leave a limit unlimited when you want the key to inherit your account allowance.
profile.shared_keys.form_actions_hint: Review limits before sharing the key ID. Changes may take a few minutes to appear in the API.
profile.shared_keys.name: Name
profile.styles.no_styles: You haven't created any styles yet
profile.styles.create_hint: Click the "Create Style" button to make your first style
profile.guides_explanation: Page guides are helpful tips and highlights that show you where to find important features on each page. You can choose to hide them if you prefer a cleaner interface, or restore them at any time if you want a refresher on where things are.
profile.delete_account_warning_30_days: Deleting your account begins a 30-day grace period during which you can undo the deletion. After 30 days, you must confirm the deletion again to make it permanent.
profile.delete_account_warning_permanent: Warning: Your account has already been marked for deletion. If 30 days have passed since you initiated deletion, confirming now will permanently delete your account. This action cannot be undone.
profile.delete_account_enter_username: To confirm, enter your username below:
profile.delete_account_success: Your account has been marked for deletion.
profile.delete_account_permanent_success: Your account has been permanently deleted.
profile.account_marked_for_deletion_mess: Your account is currently marked for deletion. After 30 days from when you initiated deletion, you can permanently delete it. If you wish to keep your account, click the button below to restore it.
profile.undelete_account_message: Are you sure you want to restore your account? This will cancel the pending deletion.
profile.change_username_desc: Your discriminator (the number after #) is your permanent horde ID and will not change. You can change your display name at any time.
profile.edit_contact_desc: This is private and only visible to horde admins. Provide a way for admins to reach you if there's an issue with your workers (e.g. email or Discord handle).
profile.contact_help_malfunction: Set a contact so admins can reach you if one of your workers malfunctions.
profile.public_workers_desc: When enabled, your worker IDs are visible to other users.
profile.public_workers_confirm_enable_de: Other users will be able to see your worker IDs.
profile.public_workers_confirm_disable_d: Your worker IDs will be hidden from other users.
profile.profile_update_error: Failed to update profile. Please try again.
profile.danger_zone_desc: Irreversible account actions. Proceed with caution.

admin.users.title: User Management
admin.users.search_placeholder: Search by username or user ID...
admin.users.select_user: Enter a username or user ID to search.
admin.users.unsaved_changes: You have unsaved changes. Click 'Save Changes' to apply them.
admin.users.hint.proxy_passkey: Pass this to your proxy to authenticate requests.
admin.users.desc.moderator: Set to true to make this user a horde moderator.
admin.users.desc.trusted: When set to true, the user and their servers will not be affected by suspicion.
admin.users.desc.flagged: When set to true, the user cannot transfer kudos and all their workers are put into permanent maintenance.
admin.users.desc.public_workers: Set to true to make this user display their worker IDs.
admin.users.desc.customizer: When set to true, the user can serve custom Stable Diffusion models which do not exist in the Official AI Horde Model Reference.
admin.users.desc.service: When set to true, the user is considered a service account proxying the requests for other users.
admin.users.desc.education: When set to true, the user is considered an education account and some options become more restrictive.
admin.users.desc.special: When set to true, the user can send special payloads.
admin.users.desc.filtered: When set to true, the replacement filter will always be applied against this user.
admin.users.desc.vpn_access: When set to true, the user will be able to onboard workers behind a VPN (temporary until trusted).
admin.users.desc.worker_invites: Set to the amount of workers this user is allowed to join when in worker invite-only mode.
admin.users.desc.usage_multiplier: The amount by which to multiply the user's kudos consumption.
admin.users.desc.concurrency: The amount of concurrent requests this user can have.
admin.users.desc.monthly_kudos: When specified, will start assigning the user monthly kudos, starting now!
admin.users.desc.worker_count: How many workers this user has created (active or inactive).
admin.users.desc.evaluating_kudos: The amount of Evaluating Kudos this untrusted user has from generations and uptime.
admin.users.desc.kudos: The amount of Kudos this user has. The amount of Kudos determines request priority.
admin.users.desc.suspicious: How much suspicion this user has accumulated.
admin.users.desc.contact: Contact details for the horde admins to reach the user in case of emergency.
admin.users.desc.admin_comment: Information about this user added by the admins.
admin.users.tracked_generations.info: Tracked generations are polled every 10 seconds for status updates.
admin.users.tracked_generations.empty: Click "Track" on an active generation ID above to monitor its status here.
admin.users.styles.name: Name
admin.users.dialog.undelete_message: Are you sure you want to restore this user? This will cancel their pending deletion.
admin.common.error: An error occurred. Please try again.
admin.common.access_denied_message: You must be logged in to access this page.

admin.operations.worker_block.worker_pla: Search for a worker by name or ID...
admin.operations.dialog.delete_ip_confir: Are you sure you want to remove this IP timeout? The IP will be unblocked immediately.
admin.operations.dialog.refresh_ip_confi: This will extend the timeout duration for this IP address.
admin.operations.dialog.block_worker_con: Are you sure you want to block this worker's IP address? This will prevent the worker from connecting to the horde.
admin.operations.dialog.unblock_worker_c: Are you sure you want to remove the IP block for this worker?
admin.operations.dialog.batch_refresh_co: This will refresh the timeout duration for all selected IP addresses.
details.leaderboard.title: Kudos Leaderboard
details.leaderboard.appreciation_title: Thank You to Our Top Contributors!
details.leaderboard.appreciation_message: These generous individuals have accumulated kudos by running workers and contributing their computing power to the AI Horde community. Their dedication and generosity make free AI generation possible for everyone. We are deeply grateful for their ongoing support!
details.leaderboard.kudos_explanation_1: Kudos are the priority currency of AI Horde, earned by running workers that process generation requests for the community. Users with more kudos get faster queue times, but kudos never expire and cannot be sold for money. They can, however, be gifted to other users.
details.leaderboard.kudos_explanation_2: High kudos balances represent individuals who have contributed far more computing power to the community than they have consumed. These are the backbone of the volunteer network: people who keep the horde running by consistently providing GPU power so that anyone can generate AI images and text for free.
details.leaderboard.learn_more_faq: Learn more about kudos in our FAQ →
details.leaderboard.footer_note: Kudos can be freely gifted between users but cannot be exchanged for money. Running a worker is the primary way to earn kudos and help the community.
details.leaderboard.find_my_rank_descrip: Discover where you stand among all AI Horde contributors.
details.leaderboard.thank_you_message: Thank you for being part of the AI Horde community!

joining.title: Join the Horde
joining.subtitle: Become a worker and help power the world's largest free, open-source AI generation network. Your GPU can make a difference.
joining.mission.title: Our Mission
joining.mission.description: The AI Horde is a non-profit, community-driven project committed to democratizing access to AI technologies. We believe everyone should have the opportunity to use generative AI, regardless of their financial means or technical resources. This service relies entirely on volunteer workers like you to make this vision a reality.
joining.why_workers.title: Why We Need Workers
joining.why_workers.description: Unlike commercial AI services that rely on expensive cloud infrastructure, the AI Horde is powered entirely by volunteers who share their computing resources. Every image generated, every text completed: it all happens because generous community members donate their GPU power. Without workers, there is no Horde. Your contribution directly enables thousands of people worldwide to access AI generation for free.
joining.worker_types.title: Choose Your Worker Type
joining.worker_types.image.title: Image Generation
joining.worker_types.image.description: Generate stunning AI images using Stable Diffusion, SDXL, Flux, and other image models. Image workers are in high demand and earn kudos quickly.
joining.worker_types.image.regen_desc: Official image worker with ComfyUI backend, supporting all major image models
joining.worker_types.text.title: Text Generation
joining.worker_types.text.description: Power text generation using Large Language Models (LLMs). Help users with creative writing, conversations, and more.
joining.worker_types.text.koboldcpp_desc: Fast, easy-to-use LLM backend with GGUF model support
joining.worker_types.text.aphrodite_desc: High-performance inference engine optimized for throughput
joining.worker_types.text.bridge_note: These backends connect to the Horde via the
joining.benefits.title: Why Become a Worker?
joining.benefits.earn_kudos.title: Earn Kudos
joining.benefits.earn_kudos.description: Accumulate kudos for every generation you process. Use them for priority access or leave them unspent to help the community.
joining.benefits.community.title: Join the Community
joining.benefits.community.description: Be part of a global movement democratizing AI access. Connect with like-minded enthusiasts on Discord.
joining.benefits.utilize_idle.title: Utilize Idle Resources
joining.benefits.utilize_idle.descriptio: Put your GPU to work when you're not using it. Convert idle computing power into community value.
joining.getting_started.title: Getting Started
joining.getting_started.step1.title: Register an Account
joining.getting_started.step1.descriptio: Create an account at aihorde.net to get your API key. This key identifies your worker and tracks your earned kudos.
joining.getting_started.step2.title: Choose Your Worker Software
joining.getting_started.step2.descriptio: Select the appropriate worker software based on whether you want to generate images or text. Follow the setup guides in the respective repositories.
joining.getting_started.step3.title: Configure Your Worker
joining.getting_started.step3.descriptio: Enter your API key, select which models you want to serve, and configure your preferences. The worker software includes detailed configuration guides.
joining.getting_started.step4.title: Start Contributing
joining.getting_started.step4.descriptio: Launch your worker and start earning kudos! Join our Discord community for support, tips, and to connect with other workers.
joining.cta.title: Ready to Make a Difference?
joining.cta.description: Join our Discord community to get help setting up your worker, share your experiences, and become part of the AI Horde family.
joining.alternative_support.title: Not able to run a worker?
joining.alternative_support.description: If you don't have a compatible GPU or can't run a worker, you can still support the AI Horde through
contribute.title: Contribute to AI Horde
contribute.subtitle: Whether you contribute computing power, development efforts, or financial support, all contributions make a difference.
workers.what_are.title: What Are Workers?
workers.what_are.description: Workers are volunteer-run software that process AI generation requests for the Horde. They poll the AI Horde API for pending jobs, generate results using your GPU, and return them to the requesting user. In return, you earn kudos, the Horde's priority currency.
workers.what_are.types_intro: There are three types of workers: Dreamers generate images, Scribes produce text, and Alchemists handle post-processing tasks like upscaling and captioning. Each worker loads one or more models, the trained AI files that determine what it can generate.
workers.diagram.aria_label: Diagram showing how AI Horde workers process requests: a user submits a request to the API, a worker polls for and processes the job, then returns the result
workers.diagram.intro: AI Horde works as a request loop. Users talk to the API, and workers repeatedly poll the API for jobs they can run.
workers.diagram.loop_hint: This cycle repeats continuously — workers never accept inbound jobs, they always ask the API for the next one.
workers.types.title: Worker Types
workers.types.image.title: Image Generation (Dreamers)
workers.types.image.description: Generate AI images using Stable Diffusion, SDXL, Flux, and other image models. Image workers are in high demand and earn kudos quickly.
workers.types.image.regen_desc: Official image worker with ComfyUI backend, supporting all major image models
workers.types.text.title: Text Generation (Scribes)
workers.types.text.description: Power text generation using Large Language Models (LLMs). Help users with creative writing, conversations, and more.
workers.types.text.koboldcpp_desc: Fast, easy-to-use LLM backend with GGUF model support
workers.types.text.aphrodite_desc: High-performance inference engine optimized for throughput
workers.types.text.bridge_note: These backends connect to the Horde via the
workers.types.alchemy.title: Post-Processing (Alchemists)
workers.types.alchemy.description: Handle post-processing tasks such as image upscaling, face fixing, captioning, and interrogation. Alchemists complement image workers by enhancing their outputs.
workers.types.alchemy.worker_desc: Official bridge supporting alchemy operations including upscaling, captioning, and interrogation
workers.faq.title: Good to Know
workers.faq.privacy.title: User Privacy
workers.faq.privacy.description: Workers never receive any identifying information about the user who submitted a request. All requests are anonymized by the API before being dispatched to workers.
workers.faq.security.title: Network Security
workers.faq.security.description: Workers only make outbound connections to poll the API for jobs. They never listen on ports or accept incoming connections, so running a worker does not expose your system to the internet.
workers.faq.trust.title: Trust System
workers.faq.trust.description: The Horde uses a trust and suspicion system to protect against abuse. Workers that consistently deliver quality results build trust, while those producing bad outputs are flagged and eventually excluded.
workers.faq.cost.title: Obligations
workers.faq.cost.description: You volunteer your GPU when it's idle and stop whenever you want. There are no costs or obligations to run a worker, and you can uninstall the software at any time. You can recover the effort you put in by spending kudos.
workers.alternative_support.title: Not able to run a worker?
workers.alternative_support.description: If you don't have a compatible GPU or can't run a worker, you can still support the AI Horde through
workers.getting_started.title: Getting Started
workers.getting_started.step1.title: Register an Account
workers.getting_started.step1.descriptio: Create an account at aihorde.net to get your API key. This key identifies your worker and tracks your earned kudos.
workers.getting_started.step2.title: Choose Your Worker Software
workers.getting_started.step2.descriptio: Pick the software for the type of work you want to do: horde-worker-reGen for images, KoboldCpp or Aphrodite for text, or AI-Horde-Worker for alchemy.
workers.getting_started.step3.title: Configure Your Worker
workers.getting_started.step3.descriptio: Enter your API key, select which models you want to serve, and configure your preferences. The worker software includes detailed configuration guides.
workers.getting_started.step4.title: Start Contributing
workers.getting_started.step4.descriptio: Launch your worker and start earning kudos! Join our Discord community for support, tips, and to connect with other workers.
workers.cta.title: Ready to Make a Difference?
workers.cta.description: Join our Discord community to get help setting up your worker, share your experiences, and become part of the AI Horde family.
workers.stats.title: Network Status
workers.stats.image.label: Image (Dreamers)
workers.stats.text.label: Text (Scribes)
workers.stats.alchemy.label: Alchemy
workers.stats.cta_message: The network needs more processing power.
workers.stats.cta_link: Read more on how to help ↓
workers.stats.read_more_to_help_link: Read more on how to help →

donate.intro.title: Support Our Infrastructure
donate.intro.description: The AI Horde relies on volunteer computing power, but we also have infrastructure costs that require financial support. Your donation helps keep the service running smoothly for everyone.
donate.what_supports.title: What Your Donation Supports
donate.what_supports.description: All donations go directly toward maintaining and improving the AI Horde infrastructure:
donate.what_supports.servers.title: Server Costs
donate.what_supports.servers.description: API servers, databases, and backend infrastructure that coordinate the network
donate.what_supports.development.title: Development
donate.what_supports.development.descrip: Ongoing development and improvements to the platform and tools
donate.what_supports.community.title: Community
donate.what_supports.community.descripti: Resources and tools to support our growing community of users and workers
donate.ways_to_donate.title: Ways to Donate
donate.ways_to_donate.haidra.title: Haidra.net
donate.ways_to_donate.haidra.description: Donate directly through the Haidra website (our preferred donation method)
donate.ways_to_donate.github_sponsors.de: Support development directly via GitHub Sponsors
donate.ways_to_donate.patreon.kudos_note: Patrons receive monthly kudos as a thank-you for their support!
donate.ways_to_donate.kofi.title: Ko-fi
donate.ways_to_donate.kofi.description: Make a one-time or recurring donation on Ko-fi
donate.transparency.title: Transparency
donate.transparency.description: Haidra, the organization behind AI Horde, is a registered non-profit association (ASBL) under Luxembourg law and is forbidden from distributing surplus to members or third parties. All donations go directly toward infrastructure costs and platform development. Our code is completely open source, and we are committed to keeping the service free for everyone.
donate.alternatives.title: Other Ways to Help
donate.alternatives.description: Financial donations aren't the only way to support AI Horde. If you have a compatible GPU, consider running a worker to contribute computing power directly to the network.
volunteer_cta.message: The workers and models you see here are provided by generous volunteers. Please consider becoming a worker or donating to support the community.
help.workers.title: What are Workers?
help.workers.subtitle: Volunteer GPUs powering the AI Horde network
help.workers.body:
Workers are volunteer computers (typically with powerful GPUs) that process AI generation requests for the entire AI Horde community. Anyone with a compatible GPU can run worker software to contribute processing power.

There are three types: Image workers (Dreamers) generate images, Text workers (Scribes) generate text, and Alchemy workers (Alchemists) handle image utilities like captioning and upscaling. Each worker loads one or more models, the trained AI files that determine what it can generate.

This page shows all currently online workers, their performance, loaded models, and capabilities. Workers earn kudos for their owners as a reward for volunteering.
help.models.title: What are Models?
help.models.subtitle: The AI brains that power each generation
help.models.body:
Models are trained AI files that workers load to generate images or text. Different models specialize in different styles, quality levels, and capabilities: for example, some excel at photorealism while others are better at anime or illustration.

The Workers column shows how many volunteer GPUs currently have this model loaded and ready. Queued shows the amount of pending work waiting. ETA is the estimated time to clear the queue. Performance shows the current generation speed across all workers running that model.

More workers on a model means faster generation times and lower queue wait times for users requesting that model.
help.stats.title: Usage Statistics
help.stats.subtitle: Real-time and historical performance of the AI Horde
help.stats.body:
This page shows the current state and historical usage of the entire AI Horde distributed network. The Overview tab shows real-time generation speeds, active worker counts, and current queue sizes.

Standard images are a normalized unit: one standard image equals a typical 1024×1024 image at 20 steps. Pages of text normalize token counts into roughly 500-word pages. These units make it easier to understand the scale of work being done.

The Image and Text tabs show per-model breakdowns, indicating which models are most popular and how the workload is distributed across available models.
help.workers.tooltip.total_workers: The number of volunteer machines currently online and processing requests for this worker type.
help.workers.tooltip.total_threads: Total parallel processing slots across all workers. A worker with 2 threads can process 2 jobs simultaneously.
help.workers.tooltip.performance: Total capacity: the combined average speed of all online workers. These averages represent the speed over the past several jobs. The actual output (Recent Throughput) is usually higher due to differences in speed between different models and parameters.
help.workers.tooltip.recent_throughput: Recent throughput: actual work the network completed in the last 60 seconds. This is the same number shown on the homepage and reflects real output, not just average worker performance.
help.workers.tooltip.capabilities: Special features this worker supports beyond basic generation. Not all workers support all capabilities.
help.workers.tooltip.worker_id: A unique identifier (UUID) for this worker. Useful for looking up a specific worker or reporting issues.
help.workers.tooltip.bridge_agent: The software connecting this volunteer's GPU to the AI Horde network. Different bridges may support different features.
help.workers.tooltip.threads: How many jobs this worker can process in parallel. More threads means higher throughput but requires more GPU memory.
help.workers.tooltip.models_loaded: AI models currently loaded on this worker. Workers with more models can handle a wider variety of requests but use more memory.
help.workers.tooltip.img2img: Supports image-to-image generation: creating new images based on an input image and a text prompt.
help.workers.tooltip.inpainting: Supports inpainting: selectively regenerating parts of an image while keeping the rest intact.
help.workers.tooltip.post_processing: Supports post-processing effects like upscaling, face fixing (GFPGAN/CodeFormers), and other image enhancements.
help.workers.tooltip.lora: Supports LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation): small add-on models that modify generation style without replacing the base model.
help.workers.tooltip.controlnet: Supports ControlNet: advanced guidance that uses reference images (edges, poses, depth maps) to control generation composition.
help.workers.tooltip.sdxl_controlnet: Supports ControlNet specifically for SDXL (Stable Diffusion XL) models.
help.workers.tooltip.nsfw: Whether this worker accepts requests marked as NSFW (Not Safe For Work). Workers with NSFW disabled will skip such requests.
help.workers.tooltip.maintenance: Worker is temporarily offline for maintenance by its owner. It will not accept new jobs until maintenance is cleared.
help.workers.tooltip.paused: Worker has been paused by a moderator and cannot accept jobs until unpaused.
help.workers.tooltip.flagged: Worker has been flagged by moderators for investigation, typically due to suspicious output or behavior.
help.workers.tooltip.trusted: Worker owner has been verified as trusted; they've run a worker reliably for at least one week.
help.models.tooltip.workers: How many volunteer GPUs currently have this model loaded and ready to process requests.
help.models.tooltip.queued: The total amount of pending work waiting for this model, measured in megapixelsteps (image) or tokens (text).
help.models.tooltip.jobs: The number of individual generation tasks currently waiting in the queue for this model.
help.models.tooltip.eta: Estimated time until the current queue for this model is cleared, based on current worker count and performance.
help.models.tooltip.performance: The combined average generation speed of all workers currently running this model.
help.stats.tooltip.realtime_performance: Live generation speeds measured across all currently active workers in the network.
help.stats.tooltip.queued_requests: Total user requests waiting to be processed. Each request may contain multiple individual jobs.
help.stats.tooltip.queued_work: The total volume of pending work, measured in native units (megapixelsteps for images, tokens for text).
help.stats.tooltip.worker_count: Number of volunteer machines currently running and connected to the AI Horde network.
help.glossary.title: AI Horde Glossary
help.glossary.page.profile.requests_made: Requests made to the horde by this account.
help.glossary.page.profile.requests_fulf: Number of requests this account's workers have fulfilled.
help.glossary.page.profile.total_fulfill: The combined total number of requests this account's workers have fulfilled.
help.glossary.page.profile.kudos_generat: Generated work from this account's workers, shown as megapixelsteps (and tokens in detailed records).
help.glossary.page.profile.accumulated.d: The total number of kudos earned over the life of the account.
help.glossary.page.profile.gifted.descri: The number of kudos transferred to other users.
help.glossary.page.profile.received.desc: The number of kudos received through transfers from other users.
help.glossary.page.profile.recurring.des: Kudos generated by the system as a result of cash donations you make to help run the service. Certain volunteers also receive these kinds of kudos, such as moderators.
help.glossary.page.profile.donated.descr: The number of kudos transferred to public-good keys, such as Educational Accounts.
help.glossary.page.workers.icon_type_ima: Worker specializes in image generation tasks. These workers typically run software like reGen and support models such as Stable Diffusion.
help.glossary.page.workers.icon_type_tex: Worker specializes in text generation tasks. These workers typically run software like KoboldCpp or Aphrodite and support various LLM models.
help.glossary.page.workers.icon_type_int: Worker specializes in post-processing tasks such as image upscaling, face fixing, captioning, and interrogation. These workers typically run the AI-Horde-Worker bridge.
help.glossary.page.workers.icon_online.d: Worker is online and is available for processing jobs.
help.glossary.page.workers.icon_issue.de: Worker may be experiencing issues affecting its performance.
help.glossary.page.workers.icon_issue_ma: Worker is in maintenance mode and will not accept new jobs.
help.glossary.page.workers.icon_issue_pa: Worker has been paused by a moderator due to suspicious activity.
help.glossary.page.workers.icon_issue_fl: Worker has been flagged by moderators for policy violations.
help.glossary.page.workers.icon_issue_mu: Worker has multiple active issues. Review the status indicators for details.
Worker is not currently connected to the horde and cannot process jobs.
help.glossary.page.workers.icon_low_spee: Worker is performing below expected speed thresholds and is considered a 'slow_worker'. Users can spend additional kudos using the `slow_workers: false` option to skip these workers and get faster results.
help.glossary.page.workers.icon_high_spe: Worker is performing above usual speed thresholds. This can be normal for high-end GPUs or certain models, but may also indicate inaccurate reporting or other anomalies.
help.glossary.page.workers.icon_paused.d: Worker has been paused by the system due for suspicious activity. Only moderators can see this.
help.glossary.page.workers.icon_maintena: Worker is in maintenance mode, it will finish current jobs but not accept new ones.
Worker has been flagged by moderators for policy violations or suspicious behavior.
Worker belongs to a trusted user. Most users become trusted after about a week of consistent activity.
help.glossary.page.workers.badge_img2img: Worker supports image-to-image generation, transforming an input image based on a prompt.
help.glossary.page.workers.badge_inpaint: Worker supports inpainting/outpainting, selectively regenerating masked areas of an image.
help.glossary.page.workers.badge_post_pr: Worker supports post-processing operations such as upscaling and face-fixing.
help.glossary.page.workers.badge_lora.de: Worker supports LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) models for fine-tuned style and subject generation.
help.glossary.page.workers.badge_control: Worker supports ControlNet for guided image generation using structural inputs like depth maps or edge detection.
help.glossary.terms.ai_horde.title: AI Horde
help.glossary.terms.ai_horde.body: A volunteer-powered distributed cluster for AI image and text generation. Instead of relying on a central server, the processing power comes from volunteers who share their GPUs.
help.glossary.terms.kudos.title: Kudos
help.glossary.terms.kudos.body: The priority system of the AI Horde. Workers earn kudos for processing jobs, and users spend kudos for higher priority in the queue. Kudos never expire and cannot be sold (but they can be gifted to other users); they represent your contribution to the community.
help.glossary.terms.request.title: Request
help.glossary.terms.request.body_images: The number of images requested over the life of the account. This can be higher than the number of requests made if some requests included multiple images (e.g. requesting 4 images counts as 1 request but 4 jobs).
help.glossary.terms.request.body_text: The number of text generations requested over the life of the account. This can be higher than the number of requests made if some requests included multiple generations (e.g. requesting 3 responses counts as 1 request but 3 jobs).
help.glossary.terms.job.title: Job
help.glossary.terms.job.body: A single unit of work derived from a request. For example, a request for 4 images becomes 4 jobs, each producing one image. Jobs are distributed to available workers.
help.glossary.terms.model.title: Model
help.glossary.terms.model.body: A trained AI file that a worker loads to perform generation. Different models produce different styles and quality; for example, Stable Diffusion for images or LLaMA for text. Workers choose which models to load.
help.glossary.terms.team.title: Team
help.glossary.terms.team.body: A group of workers organized by their owners. Teams can pool contributions and appear on the leaderboard. Joining a team is optional.
help.glossary.terms.trusted.title: Trusted
help.glossary.terms.trusted.body: A user status earned by running a worker reliably for at least one week. Trusted users get additional privileges and their workers receive priority.
help.glossary.terms.nsfw.title: NSFW Filter
help.glossary.terms.nsfw.body: Controls whether a worker accepts Not Safe For Work requests. Workers with NSFW disabled will skip such jobs. This is set by each worker's operator.
help.glossary.terms.worker.title: Worker
help.glossary.terms.worker.body: A volunteer's computer (typically with a GPU) running bridge software to process AI generation requests. Workers earn kudos for their owners.
help.glossary.terms.dreamer.title: Dreamer (Image Worker)
help.glossary.terms.dreamer.body: A worker that generates images from text prompts (text-to-image) or from other images (image-to-image). Uses models like Stable Diffusion.
help.glossary.terms.scribe.title: Scribe (Text Worker)
help.glossary.terms.scribe.body: A worker that generates text responses using large language models (LLMs). Typically requires GPUs with large amounts of VRAM.
help.glossary.terms.alchemist.title: Alchemist (Alchemy Worker)
help.glossary.terms.alchemist.body: A worker that performs image utility operations: captioning (describing images), interrogation (analyzing image content), and upscaling.
help.glossary.terms.bridge_agent.title: Bridge Agent
help.glossary.terms.bridge_agent.body: The software that connects a volunteer's GPU to the AI Horde network. It polls for jobs, runs the AI model locally, and returns results. Examples include AI Horde Worker and hordelib.
help.glossary.terms.text2img.title: Text-to-Image (txt2img)
help.glossary.terms.text2img.body: The standard generation mode: a text description (prompt) is converted into an image by the AI model through a diffusion process.
help.glossary.terms.img2img.title: Image-to-Image (img2img)
help.glossary.terms.img2img.body: A generation mode that uses an existing image as a starting point, then modifies it according to a text prompt. Useful for style transfer and guided editing.
help.glossary.terms.inpainting.title: Inpainting
help.glossary.terms.inpainting.body: Selectively regenerating a masked region of an image while keeping the surrounding area intact. Used for removing, adding, or changing specific parts of an image.
help.glossary.terms.lora.title: LoRA
help.glossary.terms.lora.body: Low-Rank Adaptation: a lightweight model add-on that modifies generation style without replacing the base model. LoRAs are small files that can add specific characters, styles, or concepts.
help.glossary.terms.controlnet.title: ControlNet
help.glossary.terms.controlnet.body: An advanced technique that uses reference images (like edge maps, depth maps, or pose skeletons) to precisely control the composition and structure of generated images.
help.glossary.terms.post_processing.body: Enhancements applied after image generation, such as upscaling (increasing resolution), face fixing (GFPGAN/CodeFormers), and other quality improvements.
help.glossary.terms.maintenance.title: Maintenance Mode
help.glossary.terms.maintenance.body: A worker state set by its owner indicating it's temporarily offline. The worker won't accept new jobs until maintenance mode is cleared.
help.glossary.terms.paused.title: Paused
help.glossary.terms.paused.body: A worker state set by moderators. A paused worker cannot accept jobs until a moderator unpauses it. This is typically used for investigation.
help.glossary.terms.flagged.title: Flagged
help.glossary.terms.flagged.body: A worker that has been flagged by moderators for investigation, typically due to suspicious output, slow performance, or rule violations.
help.glossary.terms.threads.title: Threads
help.glossary.terms.threads.body: The number of jobs a worker can process simultaneously. More threads increase throughput but require more GPU memory and compute power.
help.glossary.terms.megapixelsteps.title: Megapixelsteps (MPS)
help.glossary.terms.megapixelsteps.body: A unit measuring image generation work: megapixels × diffusion steps. One megapixel is a 1024×1024 image. A 20-step generation of a 1024×1024 image equals 20 megapixelsteps.
help.glossary.terms.tokens.title: Tokens
help.glossary.terms.tokens.body: The basic unit of text for language models. One token is roughly 0.75 words (or about 4 characters). Text generation speed is measured in tokens per second.
help.glossary.terms.eta.title: ETA (Estimated Time)
help.glossary.terms.eta.body: The estimated time until a model's queue is cleared, calculated from current queue depth divided by current processing speed.
help.glossary.terms.uptime.title: Uptime
help.glossary.terms.uptime.body: How long a worker has been continuously connected and processing jobs since it last started.
help.glossary.terms.api_key.title: API Key
help.glossary.terms.api_key.body: A secret key that identifies you to the AI Horde. You can use the anonymous key (0000000000) to try the service immediately, or register for a personal key to earn kudos and get higher priority.
help.glossary.terms.queue.title: Queue
help.glossary.terms.queue.body: When you submit a generation request, it enters a queue. Workers pick up jobs from the queue in priority order; users with more kudos are served first. Queue depth indicates how much work is waiting.
help.glossary.terms.gui.title: GUI (Frontend)
help.glossary.terms.gui.body: A graphical user interface that lets you use the AI Horde without writing code. Frontends provide visual tools for creating images or text with features like prompt editors, image galleries, and parameter controls.
help.glossary.terms.sdk.title: SDK / Library
help.glossary.terms.sdk.body: A software development kit or code library that lets programmers integrate AI Horde into their own applications. Available in various programming languages.
help.glossary.terms.cli.title: CLI Tool
help.glossary.terms.cli.body: A command-line interface tool: software you run from a terminal or command prompt rather than a graphical window. Useful for scripting and automation.
help.glossary.terms.bot.title: Bot
help.glossary.terms.bot.body: An automated program that connects AI Horde to a chat platform like Discord or Telegram, letting users generate images or text through chat commands.
help.glossary.terms.utility.title: Utility
help.glossary.terms.utility.body: A helper tool that works alongside the AI Horde, such as model browsers, prompt generators, or workflow managers. Not a full frontend, but adds useful functionality.
help.styles.title: What are Styles?
help.styles.subtitle: Reusable generation presets shared by the community
help.styles.body:
Styles are saved generation presets (a combination of prompt template, model, and parameters) that anyone can create and share. Using a style lets you reproduce a specific look or technique without manually configuring every setting.

Image styles include settings like the model, sampler, CFG scale, steps, and LoRAs. Text styles include parameters like temperature, top-p, and stop sequences. Styles can be public (visible to everyone) or private (only visible to the creator).

Browse styles by category, search by name or tag, and click any style to see its full configuration and example outputs.
help.teams.title: What are Teams?
help.teams.subtitle: Groups of workers organized by their owners
help.teams.body:
Teams let worker operators organize their volunteer GPUs into named groups. A team pools the contributions of its member workers, making it easy to track collective impact.

Each team shows its total workers, accumulated kudos (priority credits earned by processing jobs), and requests fulfilled. Teams appear on the leaderboard and provide a way for organizations or groups of friends to contribute together.

Any trusted user can create a team and assign their workers to it. Workers can only belong to one team at a time.
help.leaderboard.title: What is the Kudos Leaderboard?
help.leaderboard.subtitle: Ranking the most generous contributors to the network
help.leaderboard.body:
The Kudos Leaderboard ranks AI Horde users by their unspent kudos balance. High balances represent people who have contributed far more computing power (by running workers) than they have consumed; they are the backbone of the volunteer network.

Kudos are priority credits: workers earn them by processing generation jobs, and users spend them for faster queue times. Kudos never expire and cannot be sold for money, but they can be gifted to other users. A large balance simply means someone has been very generous with their GPU time.

You can find your own rank using the "Find Your Rank" feature if you're logged in.
help.users.title: User Lookup
help.users.subtitle: Find and view any AI Horde user's public profile
help.users.body:
Look up any AI Horde user by their numeric ID or full username (e.g., 'alias#123') to see their public profile information.

User profiles show kudos balance, worker count, account age, and contribution statistics. You can also navigate to their workers and teams from the profile page.

The Kudos Leaderboard is another way to browse top contributors.
help.profile_guest.title: Your AI Horde Profile
help.profile_guest.subtitle: View your kudos, workers, teams, and generation history
help.profile_guest.body:
Your profile shows your kudos balance (priority credits), generation history, worker statistics, and team memberships. You can also manage shared API keys and custom styles from here.

To view your profile, enter your API key, the account key you received when you registered. Your key is only used to fetch your data and is stored locally in your browser.

Don't have an account? Register for free to get an API key, earn kudos, and track your contributions. You can use AI Horde anonymously, but registered users earn kudos for higher priority.
help.home.tooltip.image_stats: Live statistics for AI image generation powered by volunteer 'Dreamer' workers. Speed shows actual network throughput (work completed in the last 60 seconds), which may differ from the total capacity shown on the workers page.
help.home.tooltip.text_stats: Live statistics for AI text generation powered by volunteer 'Scribe' workers. Speed shows actual network throughput (work completed in the last 60 seconds), which may differ from the total capacity shown on the workers page.
help.home.tooltip.alchemy: Image Alchemy covers utility operations like captioning (describing images), interrogation (analyzing content), and upscaling, all handled by 'Alchemist' workers.
help.home.tooltip.realtime_stats: Links to detailed real-time dashboards showing historical usage trends, model popularity, and network performance over time.
help.home.tooltip.quickstart: Everything you need to start generating. You can use the anonymous API key right away, with no registration required. Registering gives you kudos (priority credits) for faster results.
help.home.tooltip.try_frontend: Frontends are web apps that let you use AI Horde visually: just type a prompt and get results. No coding or setup required.
help.tools.tooltip.guis: Graphical frontends for generating images and text. These are the easiest way to use AI Horde: just open one in your browser and start creating.
help.tools.tooltip.workers: Software for volunteering your GPU to the network. Running a worker earns kudos and helps the community.
help.tools.tooltip.resources: Guides, documentation, model collections, and community resources for learning about AI Horde and AI generation.
help.tools.tooltip.utilities: Helper tools that complement frontends, such as model browsers, prompt builders, and workflow managers.
help.tools.tooltip.bots: Chat platform integrations that let you generate images or text through Discord, Telegram, and other messaging apps.
help.tools.tooltip.developer_tools: SDKs, libraries, CLI tools, and plugins for developers who want to build on the AI Horde API.
mission_callout.title: Our Mission
mission_callout.description: AI Horde is a free, community-powered service that provides open access to generative AI. We are a volunteer-run, non-profit effort dedicated to ensuring powerful machine learning technologies remain available to all people, regardless of their financial means or technical resources.
mission.title: Our Mission
mission.subtitle: AI Horde exists because we believe transformative technology should belong to everyone, not just those who can afford it.
mission.statement.title: Mission Statement
mission.statement.body: AI Horde is a free, community-powered service that provides open access to generative AI. We are a volunteer-run, non-profit effort dedicated to ensuring that powerful machine learning technologies remain available to all people, regardless of their financial means, geographic location, or technical resources. We believe that when people freely share what they have, everyone gains more than any one person gives up.
mission.why.title: Why We Exist
mission.why.paragraph1: Generative AI is among the most transformative technologies of our time. It is reshaping how people create art, write, learn, conduct research, and solve problems. But running these models demands expensive hardware: powerful GPUs that most people simply cannot afford. Left to market forces alone, access to these capabilities concentrates in the hands of well-funded corporations and the individuals wealthy enough to pay for subscriptions.
mission.why.paragraph2: We reject the premise that your economic circumstances should determine whether you can use these tools. A student in Lagos deserves the same access to AI-assisted learning as a developer in San Francisco. An independent artist should not be priced out of creative tools that could transform their work. A researcher at an underfunded university should not have to choose between their budget and their curiosity.
mission.why.paragraph3: AI Horde was built to break that pattern. In the tradition of volunteer computing projects like Folding@home and SETI@home, we connect people who have spare computing power with people who need it. The result is a distributed cluster that belongs to no single entity, a shared resource sustained by collective generosity.
mission.values.title: What We Stand For
mission.values.access.title: Universal Access, No Exceptions
mission.values.access.description: Everyone can use AI Horde for free, even anonymously. No credit card, no paywall, and no way to buy priority. AI Horde is a registered non-profit, and our kudos system is designed so that priority can only be earned by contributing compute, not purchased. Access to transformative technology is not a privilege.
mission.values.solidarity.title: Mutual Aid
mission.values.solidarity.description: Our entire model depends on people choosing to help strangers. Volunteers donate their idle compute so that others can create, learn, and build. Most earned kudos go unspent: a quiet, ongoing act of generosity.
mission.values.openness.title: Open Source, Open Knowledge
mission.values.openness.description: We exclusively use and promote open-source models and software. Machine learning was built on decades of shared research, and we believe it should stay open. Education, art, science, and the public good all depend on it.
mission.track_record.title: The Record So Far
mission.track_record.intro: These are not aspirations. AI Horde has been doing this, continuously, since 2022.
mission.track_record.scale.paragraph1: AI Horde has generated over 170 million images and fulfilled more than 250 million text requests, entirely through volunteer-donated compute. On any given day, tens of thousands of images and hundreds of thousands of text completions pass through a distributed network of volunteer GPUs around the world.
mission.track_record.engineering.paragra: The Haidra organisation maintains over 30 open-source repositories covering the core service, worker software, model catalogues, developer tools, and more. Over 300 releases have been shipped through the efforts of 100+ contributors. Everything is publicly available, auditable, and designed to be extended by anyone.
mission.track_record.community.paragraph: The real measure of decentralisation is not the software we maintain - it is the ecosystem built around it. Dozens of independent front ends, GUIs, bots, and integrations have been created by developers outside the core team. The Haidra team are stewards of the centralised infrastructure. The community that has grown around it is the real story.
mission.crowdsourcing.title: The Power of Crowdsourcing
mission.crowdsourcing.paragraph1: Crowdsourcing only works when people are willing to give without guaranteed return. Every volunteer who runs a worker is choosing to spend their own electricity, wear on their own hardware, and dedicate their own time, not for payment, but because they believe others should have access to these tools too.
mission.crowdsourcing.paragraph2: This is not a business model. It is an act of solidarity. The volunteers who power AI Horde come from all over the world, united by a shared conviction that technology should serve people, not extract from them. Some run workers around the clock. Others contribute during off-hours when their machines would otherwise sit idle. All of them make the service possible.
mission.crowdsourcing.paragraph3: The kudos system ensures fairness without introducing money. Workers earn kudos by fulfilling requests, and those kudos grant priority for their own future requests. But kudos can never be bought or sold. They represent contributed effort, and nothing else. Those who leave their kudos unspent are effectively donating their compute to anonymous users and newcomers who have none, ensuring the system remains open even to people who have nothing to give back yet.
mission.free_access.title: Why Free Access Matters
mission.free_access.paragraph1: Commercial AI services impose subscriptions, usage caps, and terms that can change without notice. They decide what you can generate, what models you can use, and what happens to your data. When access depends on payment, entire populations are excluded: those in developing nations, students, hobbyists, independent researchers, and anyone living under economic hardship.
mission.free_access.paragraph2: Free access is not just a convenience. It is a statement about who gets to participate in the future. Generative AI is a general-purpose tool with applications across art, education, accessibility, science, and personal expression. Allowing that access to be gated by ability to pay means accepting that these benefits are only for the privileged.
mission.free_access.paragraph3: AI Horde ensures that a baseline of access always exists: one that no corporation can revoke, no terms change can eliminate, and no price increase can put out of reach. As long as volunteers are willing to share, the service endures.
mission.hedge.title: What We Guard Against
mission.hedge.paragraph1: The centralization of AI behind corporate paywalls poses a real threat to the open ecosystem that made these technologies possible in the first place. Large language models and image generators were built on openly published research, publicly available datasets, and community-driven development. When the resulting tools are locked behind subscription fees, the public effectively pays twice: once through the shared knowledge that trained the models, and again for the privilege of using them.
mission.hedge.paragraph2: We also guard against the erosion of choice. When a handful of providers control access to generative AI, they also control what content can be created, which use cases are permitted, and whose values are encoded into the system. A decentralized, volunteer-driven alternative ensures that no single entity holds that power.
mission.hedge.paragraph3: AI Horde is not opposed to commercial AI services existing. But we believe a non-commercial alternative must also exist: one that is accountable to its community rather than to shareholders, and one that prioritizes public benefit over revenue.
mission.commitments.title: Our Commitments
mission.commitments.nonprofit.title: Non-Profit, Now and Always
mission.commitments.nonprofit.descriptio: Haidra, the organization behind AI Horde, is a registered non-profit association (ASBL) under Luxembourg law. No surplus is ever distributed to members or third parties. All funds go directly toward sustaining and improving the service for the public.
mission.commitments.opensource.title: Open Source First
mission.commitments.opensource.descripti: All AI Horde software is open source. We use and promote open-source models exclusively. Closed-source models do not align with our vision of democratized access. Transparency is not optional. It is foundational.
mission.commitments.nocommodification.de: Kudos cannot be bought or sold. Priority is earned through contribution, not payment. We will never introduce a paid tier, a premium queue, or any mechanism that lets money buy faster access. The service is free, and that is a promise.
mission.commitments.community.title: Community Governance
mission.commitments.community.descriptio: AI Horde is shaped by its community. Decisions about the service are made transparently, with input from the volunteers, developers, and users who sustain it. The people who build and power the system have a voice in how it evolves.
mission.haidra.title: About Haidra
mission.haidra.paragraph1: Haidra is the volunteer organization behind AI Horde. Registered as a non-profit association in Luxembourg, Haidra's objectives include increasing access to free and open-source machine learning technologies, supporting education, facilitating open development, and promoting the free flow of information. Our articles of association explicitly state that the Association's actions shall never unfairly benefit private interests over public ones.
mission.haidra.paragraph2: The name reflects what we are: a collective of developers, operators, advocates, and users working together because we believe these tools should exist in the commons. We are not a company. We have no investors, no revenue targets, and no exit strategy. We are here because this work matters.
mission.cta.title: Join Us
mission.cta.description: AI Horde is made possible by people who believe technology should serve everyone. Whether you have a spare GPU, a few dollars, or simply want to use what the community has built, you are welcome here.
notification.need_workers.tooltip: The network needs volunteer {{types}} workers. Consider contributing!
